glossary_snr_text: Difference between useful signal and noise in dB. Higher = better. Minimum ~6 dB for a stable connection. DLM trades SNR margin for higher sync rate.
glossary_att_text: Signal loss on the copper line in dB. Depends on line length and quality. Barely changes, increase indicates physical defect.
glossary_fec_text: Automatically corrected transmission errors. Many FEC errors are normal. Only a sharp increase per hour indicates line stress.
glossary_crc_text: Uncorrectable errors — the critical ones. Cause packet loss and resyncs. CRC > 0/h is a warning sign.
glossary_dlm_text: Provider-side profile control. After reset, starts conservatively (high SNR, low rate) and gradually increases over days/weeks.
glossary_resync_text: Renegotiation of the DSL connection. Recognizable by uptime reset. Daily forced disconnect (~24h) is normal, more frequent resyncs indicate problems.
glossary_profile_text: Ratio of current sync rate to contracted bandwidth in percent. Shows how far DLM has increased the line profile. 100% = full contracted speed reached.
